Resolves #11671

Authored by GPT-5 (Codex Desktop). Session d13c94dd-e721-4e28-ac9e-4d0b3c0f66de.
FAIR-band: in-band [12/30]

Adds a compact pointer to AGENTS.md critical gate 7 so operator-suppressed AGENT:* broadcast incidents route to the existing direct-DM fallback instead of being misread as a halt-state. AGENT:* remains canonical outside explicit operator suppression; full fallback detail stays in peer-role and post-review-pickup.

Evidence: L1 (static substrate diff + source-of-authority grep + size/lint checks) -> L1 required (docs-only turn-loaded substrate pointer). No residuals.

## Deltas from ticket
None. The implementation is intentionally narrower than the ticket candidate wording: one Critical Gate line changed, no skill payload changes.

## Slot Rationale
- Modified surface: AGENTS.md §critical_gates invariant 7.
- Disposition: keep + compress-to-trigger pointer.
- 3-axis rationale: trigger-frequency is high because invariant 7 governs every tracked edit; failure-severity is high because false halt-states block valid lane pickup under operator-suppressed broadcast; enforceability remains discipline-first today, with machine-enforceable-candidate framing preserved from ADR 0007. Detailed fallback rules remain in .agents/skills/peer-role/references/peer-role-mode.md and .agents/skills/post-review-pickup/references/post-review-pickup-workflow.md.
- Decay mitigation: this is a one-line pointer into existing atlas payloads, not a duplicated fallback matrix. Retirement condition: remove the pointer if lane-claim tooling can mechanically select AGENT:* vs authorized direct DM from operator constraints.

- git diff --cached --check
- npm run ai:check-substrate-size
- npm run ai:lint-agents
- rg -n "operator.*suppress|direct-DM fallback|broadcast-suppress|AGENT:\*|lane-claim" AGENTS.md .agents/skills/peer-role/references/peer-role-mode.md .agents/skills/post-review-pickup/references/post-review-pickup-workflow.md
